justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Unstructured logging flagged with specific gaps — no JSON, no correlation IDs, no structured fields; (2) Missing /health endpoint explicitly flagged in escalations and resilience assessment; (3) Missing metrics comprehensively flagged — no sync duration, error rate, item count, or upstream latency metrics; (4) Missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as reliability risk with specific recommendation; (5) Silent failure on API outage flagged — no retry, no alert, stale data goes undetected; (6) Verdict is NOT READY; (7) Recommendations are actionable and specific throughout (e.g., '30-second timeout', 'retry with exponential backoff 3 attempts 1s/2s/4s', 'wrap DB write in transaction', 'add last_successful_sync_at timestamp').

observations.text: Verdict is unambiguously NOT READY with clear blocking gaps enumerated
All five required gaps identified plus additional valid ones (overlapping syncs, partial data corruption, missing transactions)
Recommendations are highly specific and actionable — timeout values, retry counts, metric names, alert thresholds
Correctly distinguishes must-haves from optimizations — circuit breaker listed but not treated as a blocker on par with timeout/health/metrics
Escalations correctly note that without metrics, operational readiness cannot be confirmed post-deploy
Additional valid gaps identified beyond rubric: overlapping sync runs, missing DB transactions, no schema validation on upstream response
Minor: recommended 30-second timeout is arguably generous for a stock sync API — 10s would be more standard, but this is a judgment call not a rubric violation

trials.justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Unstructured console.log flagged as insufficient — explicitly calls out no JSON output, no log levels, no correlation IDs. (2) Missing /health endpoint flagged — noted in escalations and operational_readiness. (3) Missing metrics flagged comprehensively — sync_success_total, sync_duration_seconds, last_successful_sync_timestamp all called out. (4) Missing timeout on outbound API call flagged — dedicated failure mode with specific 10s recommendation. (5) Silent failure on API outage flagged — notes single attempt with silent failure and no alerting. (6) Verdict is NOT READY with clear must-fix items. (7) Recommendations are highly actionable and specific throughout.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough gap identification — found all 5 required gaps plus additional valid ones (no payload validation, no mutex for overlapping syncs, no transaction on DB writes).
Verdict is definitively NOT READY with deployment_ready: false, not hedged as READY WITH CAVEATS.
Recommendations are concrete and specific: '10-second timeout', 'structured JSON logger', '/health endpoint returning sync status and last_successful_sync_at'.
Correctly distinguishes must-haves (health check, timeout, metrics) from optimizations (circuit breaker listed but framed as additional protection, not the core blocker).
Escalations section correctly notes that without metrics, operational readiness cannot be confirmed post-deploy — highest-priority blocker.
Goes beyond minimum by identifying additional real gaps: no payload validation, no transaction safety, no overlap protection — all valid and not fabricated.
Minor: escalates all three items to Coach K rather than forming a fully self-contained verdict, though the verdict itself is clear and well-supported.

trials.justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) console.log flagged as insufficient with specific gaps — no structured fields, no correlation ID, no log levels, no duration; (2) missing /health endpoint explicitly flagged as an escalation and in new_infrastructure_required; (3) metrics coverage marked 'missing' with detailed enumeration of needed metrics (sync_duration, records_processed, failures_total, last_success_timestamp, etc.); (4) missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as 'highest-priority fix' and 'critical production blocker'; (5) silent failure on API outage flagged — 'fails silently and waits for the next 5-min cycle'; (6) verdict is NOT READY; (7) recommendations are highly actionable and specific — '30-second HTTP timeout', 'retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s)', 'Add /health endpoint', structured logging with named fields.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough output — goes well beyond the minimum pass criteria with detailed failure mode analysis, overlapping-run risks, and empty-response data-loss scenarios.
Clear distinction between must-haves and nice-to-haves: timeout and health endpoint are 'production blockers' while circuit breaker is framed as an optimization.
Recommendations are concrete and actionable: specific timeout values, retry counts with backoff durations, exact metric names, and environment variable suggestions.
Escalations are well-structured, each with a clear reason explaining business impact (revenue, customer-facing stock errors, data loss).
Debugging capability section vividly illustrates the 3am on-call scenario, making the observability gaps tangible rather than abstract.
Correctly identifies gaps not in the rubric but genuinely present: no response validation, no overlap guard, no transaction safety — all legitimate concerns without being fabricated.
